name: Varuna
description: Varuna is a prominent deity in the early Vedic pantheon, holding a position of immense power and cosmic authority. Often associated with Mitra, he embodies the principle of Ṛta, the cosmic order and moral law that governs the universe. Varuna is the guardian of this law, ensuring its adherence and punishing transgressions. He is depicted as an all-seeing, all-knowing sovereign, possessing divine vision that penetrates all aspects of existence. His power extends over the waters, both celestial and terrestrial, making him the lord of oceans, rivers, and rain. Varuna's influence gradually diminished in later Vedic and post-Vedic Hinduism as other deities such as Indra and Vishnu rose in prominence. However, his role as a keeper of cosmic order and dispenser of justice remains a significant aspect of his character, and his connection to the waters continues to be acknowledged.
subtitle: Guardian of Cosmic Order

keyMyths.title: Varuna and the Creation of the Universe
keyMyths.description: According to some Vedic hymns, Varuna played a crucial role in the creation of the universe. He is said to have measured out the earth and the sky, establishing the boundaries and dimensions of the cosmos. This act of measurement symbolizes his role as the establisher of order from chaos. He is also credited with creating the rivers and seas, providing sustenance and life-giving water to the world. This myth highlights Varuna's power as a creator and his control over the natural world.

keyMyths.title: The Binding of Harishchandra
keyMyths.description: King Harishchandra, known for his unwavering adherence to truth, was tested by Varuna. Harishchandra promised Varuna a sacrifice if he were granted a son. When a son was born, Harishchandra delayed the sacrifice, leading to Varuna afflicting the child with a disease. This myth demonstrates Varuna's commitment to justice and the consequences of breaking promises. It emphasizes the importance of upholding one's word and honoring commitments made to the divine.

keyMyths.title: Varuna and the All-Seeing Eye
keyMyths.description: Varuna is often described as having an all-seeing eye, which surveys the entire universe. This eye represents his omniscience and his ability to monitor the actions of all beings. Nothing can escape Varuna's gaze, and he is aware of even the most secret transgressions. This myth highlights Varuna's role as a moral judge and the enforcer of cosmic law. It also emphasizes the importance of ethical conduct and the consequences of immoral behavior, as Varuna will inevitably witness and punish wrongdoing.

sources.author: Jan Gonda
sources.work: Die Religionen Indiens, I: Veda und älterer Hinduismus
sources.description: A comprehensive overview of Vedic religion, including a detailed analysis of Varuna's role and significance.
sources.text: A comprehensive overview of Vedic religion, including a detailed analysis of Varuna's role and significance.

sources.author: David Kinsley
sources.work: Hindu Goddesses: Visions of the Divine Feminine in the Hindu Religious Tradition
sources.description: While primarily focused on goddesses, it provides context for understanding the broader Vedic pantheon and Varuna's place within it.
sources.text: While primarily focused on goddesses, it provides context for understanding the broader Vedic pantheon and Varuna's place within it.

sources.author: A.A. Macdonell
sources.description: A classic work on Vedic mythology offering detailed descriptions of various deities, including Varuna, and their associated myths and rituals.
